title: Social class and educational attainment
summary: 9699 — class differences in achievement, material/cultural deprivation, and school processes.
learningObjectives: Outline class gaps in exam results and progression.
Explain material and cultural deprivation theories.
Analyse in-school processes: streaming, labelling, habitus.
Evaluate policies aimed at reducing class inequality.

simpleExplanation.title: Class still shapes grades
simpleExplanation.summary: Working-class pupils often underachieve due to poverty, lack of cultural capital, and school practices that favour middle-class norms.

flashcards.back: Poverty — poor housing, nutrition, equipment limiting learning.
Working-class language less abstract — may disadvantage in exams.
Middle-class tastes, knowledge, contacts convert to school success.
Internalised class dispositions shaping attitudes to education.
EPAs, Sure Start — state tries to offset deprivation.
Schools label middle-class knowledge 'valid' — class bias in curriculum.

sections.content: **Class and attainment** (6.2) is a Paper 3 favourite. Balance **external (home)** vs **internal (school)** factors — strongest answers use **Bourdieu**.

sections.question: Assess sociological explanations of working-class underachievement in education. [15 marks]
sections.solution:
**External — material:** Poor **housing**, **diet**, inability to afford tutors/technology.

**External — cultural:** Bernstein **language codes**; Douglas — **parental interest** linked to class; Bourdieu — **cultural capital**.

**Internal — school:** Becker **labelling**; streaming; **habitus** mismatch with school norms.

**Criticisms:** **Cultural deprivation** blames victims — **working-class culture valid**; **Asian achievement** challenges simple deprivation; **material** factors strongest evidence.

**Evaluation:** **Multi-causal** — combine **class inequality in society** with **school reproduction** (Bowles & Gintis).

**Conclusion:** Underachievement mainly **socially structured**, not lack of ability.

sections.label: Mark a social class attainment question
